text_blocks.text: Following a lengthy legal battle
with the European Commission, which deemed
the exclusivity of the rights to be against the
interests of competition and the consumer,
BSkyB's monopoly came to an end from the
2007-08 season. In May 2006, the Irish
broadcaster Setanta Sports was awarded two
of the six Premier League packages that the
English FA offered to broadcasters. Sky
picked up the remaining four for PS1.3bn. In
February 2015, Sky bid PS4.2bn for a
package of 120 premier league games across
the three seasons from 2016. This represented
an increase of 70% on the previous contract
and was said to be PS1bn more than the
company had expected to pay. The move has
been followed by staff cuts, increased
subscription prices (including 9% in Sky's
family package) and the dropping of the 3D
channel.
